findings.description: Three distinct editorial rules in the source (inclusive language/patient terminology, number formatting, Oxford comma punctuation) have no corresponding candidate rule at all. Only 2 of 6 source bullets are represented in the candidate set, leaving major content ungoverned.

rules.note: must not use 'patients' in patient materials; use 'someone/people with dermatomyositis' and 'care team' instead of 'staff'; exception: 'patients' allowed in HCP materials
spell out zero-nine, numerals for 10+; always numerals for percentages/ages/ratios; % sign for percentages
always use Oxford/serial comma in series of 3+

findings.description: u0002 introduces a distinct [GENERAL] governance-scoped requirement (each standalone section must independently establish the DM abbreviation at first mention, unless an assembly brief states otherwise) with a conditional exception. This scope/exception facet is not captured anywhere in the DM abbreviation rule, which only reflects u0001's headline/running-copy distinction.

add_facet.exception: unless assembly brief states abbreviation is established upstream

findings.description: Rule captures general tone guidance but omits the concrete governance detail that it applies specifically 'when citing clinical data' and includes a worked example pattern (VALOR study/LISRAYA/DM symptoms) that anchors the required phrasing style — this scoping context and example are not reflected in the rule's facets.

add_facet.example: Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo.

findings.description: u0001 and u0002 are structurally linked (u0002 is an indented sub-bullet clarifying assembly-time application of u0001's rule) but no relation between the two content units is preserved in the candidate rule set beyond a single merged rule; the conditional/exception nature of u0002 risks being lost or flattened.
